title: Email notifications arriving hours late
body: Task assignment notifications used to arrive within a minute or two. For the past week, they've been arriving 2-4 hours late. This is causing people to miss deadlines because they don't know they've been assigned a task until hours later. It seems to be worse in the morning — notifications sent around 9-10am take the longest to arrive. Afternoon notifications are usually faster but still delayed.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks for the detailed report — the morning pattern you noticed is a really useful clue.

I'd like to understand the broader context: you mentioned this started about a week ago. Can you think of anything that changed around that time — new team members added, a shift in how tasks are being assigned (e.g., bulk assignments in the morning), or any changes to your notification settings or email setup? And is this something your whole team is experiencing, or have you only confirmed it on your end so far?

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: Yeah, the whole team is experiencing it — I've checked with a few people and they're all seeing the same delays in the morning. I haven't changed any notification settings on my end, and I don't think we added a ton of new team members or anything like that. I do remember there was some kind of new email digest feature that started around the same time — we started getting these daily marketing/summary emails in the morning. I'm not sure if that's related but the timing lines up. We haven't changed how we assign tasks though, it's the same workflow as before.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
That's a really interesting connection with the email digest feature — the timing lining up is worth exploring.

Can you tell me more about how the delayed notifications arrive? Specifically, when they finally show up 2-4 hours late, do they come through as individual emails (one per task assignment, like they always did), or are they getting bundled or batched together in some way? And do you know if that new daily digest email is a TaskFlow feature, or is it something from a separate email/marketing tool?

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: When they finally arrive, they come through as individual emails just like they always did — one per task assignment. They're not bundled or anything, they're just late. And I'm pretty sure the digest email is a TaskFlow feature — it showed up in the app's release notes or changelog, I think around a week ago when this all started. It's like a daily summary of project activity that lands in our inbox in the morning.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body: [RESOLVED] {"title":"Email notifications delayed 2-4 hours since daily digest feature launch","problem":"Task assignment email notifications that previously arrived within 1-2 minutes are now delayed 2-4 hours. The delays are worst for notifications sent in the morning (9-10am) and affect the entire team. This started approximately one week ago, coinciding with the launch of a new daily digest/summary email feature in TaskFlow.","root_cause_hypothesis":"The new daily digest feature likely shares the same email sending queue or infrastructure as transactional notifications. When the digest emails are generated and sent each morning, they flood the queue and push time-sensitive task assignment notifications behind a large batch of lower-priority digest emails. This explains why morning delays are worst (digest sends in the morning) and afternoon notifications are faster (digest backlog has cleared).","reproduction_steps":["Assign a task to a user in the morning (around 9-10am) when the daily digest emails are being sent","Observe that the assignment notification email arrives 2-4 hours late","Repeat the same assignment in the afternoon and observe a shorter (but still present) delay","Check the email queue depth and processing times around the morning digest window"],"environment":"Affects all team members. Began approximately one week ago with the release of the daily digest feature. Not specific to any one user's settings or email provider.","severity":"high","impact":"Entire team is missing task assignment deadlines because they don't learn about assignments for hours. Directly affects team productivity and deadline adherence.","recommended_fix":"Investigate the email sending infrastructure for queue contention between digest emails and transactional notifications. Likely fixes: (1) separate the transactional notification queue from the bulk digest queue so they don't compete, (2) prioritize transactional emails over digest emails in the queue, or (3) rate-limit or schedule digest sends to avoid flooding the queue during peak notification hours.","proposed_test_case":"Send a task assignment notification while a daily digest batch is actively being processed. Verify the assignment notification is delivered within 2 minutes regardless of digest queue depth.","information_gaps":["Exact time the daily digest emails are sent each morning","Whether the email system uses a single queue or separate queues for different email types","Exact number of users/projects receiving digest emails (scale of the digest send)"]}
